tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma

tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Laboratory results are overall within normal limits, with good glycemic control and preserved renal function. Mildly elevated chloride and slightly decreased total CO₂ may suggest mild metabolic acidosis or compensation, but without other abnormalities, this is likely not clinically significant. Lipid profile and urine microalbumin/creatinine ratio are unremarkable. No acute concerns from a laboratory perspective; recommend clinical correlation.

display.value: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

tr.td: Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
